title: Cross-layer: deduplicate background process registry and shell invocation
summary: Issue #600 tracks duplicated background process registry/state behavior and duplicated shell invocation construction across agent-core, agent-runtime, and agent-platform. agent-core already re-exports the runtime background registry/state implementation, but agent-platform still carries copied registry/state code and shell argv construction remains repeated in several live execution paths.

knownCurrentState.agentRuntimeCanonicalRegistry: packages/agent-runtime/src/backgroundProcessRegistry.ts owns the full BackgroundProcessRegistry implementation and accepts an injectable spawnFn for tests.
knownCurrentState.agentRuntimeCanonicalState: packages/agent-runtime/src/background/state.ts supports both WeakMap ownership and registryId reuse to avoid orphaning registries when options objects are recreated.
knownCurrentState.agentPlatformDuplication: packages/agent-platform/src/harness/backgroundProcessRegistry.ts still carries a near copy of the runtime registry without spawnFn injection, and packages/agent-platform/src/harness/agenticTools/background/state.ts lacks runtime registryId behavior.
knownCurrentState.shellDuplication: Shell selection and argv construction are repeated in agent-core session command execution, agent-core foreground bash tool execution, agent-platform foreground bash tool execution, and background process spawning.

existingInfrastructureToPrefer: existing Vitest package tests for agent-core, agent-runtime, and agent-platform
reuseAuditSeed.newInfrastructureConstraint: Do not introduce a second registry or package-level shell helper if the runtime package can own the canonical helper without reversing dependency direction.

acceptanceFocus: agent-platform consumes the canonical @a5c-ai/agent-runtime background registry/state behavior instead of maintaining full copied implementations.
Compatibility re-export shims remain for public or known deep-import paths in agent-core and agent-platform.

A shared shell invocation helper/contract is used by agent-core session command execution, agent-core bash tool foreground/background execution, agent-platform bash tool foreground/background execution, and background process spawning.
Windows shell invocation remains cmd.exe with /c and POSIX shell invocation remains /bin/bash with -c.
cwd, env merging, timeout, stream capture, onComplete, max concurrency, cancel, killAll, dispose, and registryId reuse behavior are preserved or explicitly covered by tests.
Docs that currently warn about copied shell invocation or copied registries are updated to match the new architecture.
Endpoint resolution and SDK plugin registry duplication are assessed only as related context and are not implemented in this PR unless unavoidable.
nonGoals: Do not redesign endpoint resolution across agent-core and agent-mux.
Do not unify the SDK plugin registry with the agent-platform plugin system in this issue.
Do not change the user-facing bash tool contract beyond removing internal duplication.
Do not change shell quoting semantics, shell:false behavior, or the choice of /bin/bash on non-Windows as part of this refactor.
Do not remove compatibility exports for backgroundProcessRegistry or background/state without maintainer approval.

plannedPhases.name: Issue context
plannedPhases.purpose: Read issue #600, comments, labels, and directly related docs to lock scope and non-goals.

plannedPhases.name: Reuse audit
plannedPhases.purpose: Search existing registry/state/shell infrastructure before introducing any new helper, and render reuse-audit findings for review.

plannedPhases.name: Runtime call-path trace
plannedPhases.purpose: Trace foreground bash, background bash, session executeCommand, registry lifecycle, exports, and deep-import compatibility before modifications.

plannedPhases.name: Regression tests
plannedPhases.purpose: Add failing tests first for shared shell argv construction, platform adoption of runtime background state, lifecycle behavior, registryId reuse, and compatibility import paths.

plannedPhases.name: Design review
plannedPhases.purpose: Challenge package-boundary and compatibility assumptions before implementation begins.

plannedPhases.name: Focused refactor
plannedPhases.purpose: Convert duplicated platform registry/state to runtime imports or shims and centralize shell invocation construction in one shared contract.

plannedPhases.name: Verification loop
plannedPhases.purpose: Run targeted tests, package builds/tests, duplication scans, and iterate up to three attempts if verification or review fails.

plannedPhases.name: Compatibility review
plannedPhases.purpose: Review public exports, deep imports, Windows/POSIX argv semantics, lifecycle behavior, and docs consistency.

plannedPhases.name: Final acceptance
plannedPhases.purpose: Confirm all acceptance criteria are met, no unapproved scope expansion occurred, and release-note level summary is available.

breakpoints.when: Only if implementation discovers a real public API or dependency-direction conflict, such as needing to remove a known deep import or expose a new runtime helper through package exports.

qualityGates: Regression tests are authored before implementation and fail for the expected pre-fix reason.
Runtime call paths are recorded as runtimeCallPaths in process output.
No full copied BackgroundProcessRegistry or background state helper remains in agent-platform when a runtime import or shim is sufficient.
Only one production shell selection/argv construction contract remains for command execution paths covered by the issue.
Compatibility shims continue to resolve for agent-core and agent-platform background registry/state imports.
Targeted Vitest tests for agent-runtime, agent-core, and agent-platform pass.
Package builds for agent-runtime, agent-core, and agent-platform pass.
Documentation warnings in docs/agent-layer-gaps.md and docs/here-be-dragons.md are no longer stale after the refactor.
Compatibility review approves Windows/POSIX behavior, lifecycle behavior, package boundaries, and public exports.
verificationCommands: npm exec --yes --package=vitest -- vitest run --config packages/agent-core/vitest.config.ts packages/agent-core/src/tools.test.ts packages/agent-core/src/session.test.ts

processLibraryResearch.shapeSelected: Flat phase list because the issue is a well-defined refactor with known affected files rather than an unknown-cause forensic bug.
